### Cool Stuff & Try This
**[AI Catches Its Own "Daydreams" Before They Fool You]: The Decoder**  
Large language models (think of them as super-smart chatbots like ChatGPT) sometimes "hallucinate," which means they make up facts that aren't true, like confidently saying the sky is green. Researchers found these hallucinations leave behind measurable clues in the AI's internal math, like spilled energy from a messy calculation, and they've created a way to detect them without extra training. ### Quick Bits
**[AI Gets Smarter at Math and Screens]**  
Microsoft released Phi-4-Reasoning-Vision-15B, a compact AI model that handles images and text for tasks like solving science problems or understanding app interfaces—it's like a pocket tutor for homework. This is awesome because it shows AI shrinking down to help with real student stuff without needing huge computers.  
Source: https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/03/06/microsoft-releases-phi-4-reasoning-vision-15b-a-compact-multimodal-mod
